generated_oml:
// C3: Physical Sensing Components
instance LidarSensor : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "LiDAR sensor capturing 2D plane representations of point clouds for object detection and path projection in autonomous vehicle systems."
    DTDFVocab:producedData LidarPointCloudData
]

instance MotorServoSensor : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "Sensor monitoring motor actuation and steering servo angles for vehicle state tracking and control."
    DTDFVocab:producedData VehicleStateData
]

instance BatteryHealthSensor : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "Sensor measuring battery voltage, current, and temperature profiles for state-of-charge/health monitoring."
    DTDFVocab:producedData BatteryStateData
]

instance EnvironmentalSensor : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "Sensor capturing temperature, humidity, and environmental conditions affecting vehicle performance."
    DTDFVocab:producedData EnvironmentalData
]

// C4: Physical-to-Virtual Interaction
instance LidarPointCloudData : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    base:desc "Raw 2D plane point cloud data from LiDAR sensor transmitted at high frequency for real-time object tracking."
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom LidarSensor
]

instance VehicleStateData : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    base:desc "Structured data containing motor actuation values and steering servo angles transmitted for vehicle state synchronization."
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom MotorServoSensor
]

instance BatteryStateData : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    base:desc "High-resolution battery voltage/current/temperature profiles transmitted for degradation modeling."
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom BatteryHealthSensor
]

instance EnvironmentalData : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    base:desc "Environmental metrics (temperature, humidity) transmitted for performance prediction adjustments."
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom EnvironmentalSensor
]

instance ClusteredObjectData : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    base:desc "Processed object tracks and driving path projections derived from raw LiDAR point clouds via Point Cloud Clustering & Tracking."
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom LidarSensor
]

// C10: Models/Data
instance PointCloudClusteringModel : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "Machine learning model for converting raw LiDAR point clouds into structured object tracks and driving path projections."
    DTDFVocab:inputTo PointCloudPreProcessingEnabler
]

instance VehicleStateModel : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "Physics-based model representing vehicle dynamics and control parameters for state prediction."
    DTDFVocab:inputTo DataTranslationEnabler
]

instance BatteryDegradationModel : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "Equivalent circuit model combined with neural network for predicting battery state-of-health and remaining useful life."
    DTDFVocab:inputTo BatteryAnalyticsEnabler
]

instance EnvironmentalImpactModel : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "Physics-informed neural network predicting vehicle performance under varying environmental conditions."
    DTDFVocab:inputTo EnvironmentalAnalyticsEnabler
]

instance ProcessOptimizationModel : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "Multi-objective optimization model for adjusting manufacturing process parameters to improve yield and reduce downtime."
    DTDFVocab:inputTo ManufacturingOptimizationEnabler
]

instance OperationalMonitoringData : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Real-time operational data including equipment health, process efficiency metrics, and anomaly alerts."
    DTDFVocab:fromData LidarPointCloudData, VehicleStateData, BatteryStateData
    DTDFVocab:inputTo DigitalTwinEntity
]

instance PredictiveAnalyticsData : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Predictive insights including failure forecasts, maintenance scheduling recommendations, and performance degradation trends."
    DTDFVocab:fromData ClusteredObjectData, BatteryStateData
    DTDFVocab:inputTo PredictiveMaintenanceService
]

instance PerformanceOptimizationData : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Comparative analysis data between actual OME performance and its digital representation for process optimization."
    DTDFVocab:fromData OperationalMonitoringData
    DTDFVocab:inputTo ProcessOptimizationEnabler
]

// C11: Enablers
instance PointCloudPreProcessingEnabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Functional Entity processing raw LiDAR point clouds into standardized object tracks and driving path projections using Point Cloud Clustering & Tracking."
    DTDFVocab:enables ObjectTrackingService, PathProjectionService
]

instance DataTranslationEnabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "ISO 23247-compliant Functional Entity standardizing and aligning data streams from various sensors for digital twin integration."
    DTDFVocab:enables DataStandardizationService, DataSerializationService
]

instance BatteryAnalyticsEnabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Functional Entity processing battery state data through degradation models to generate health predictions and maintenance alerts."
    DTDFVocab:enables BatteryHealthMonitoringService, DegradationPredictionService
]

instance EnvironmentalAnalyticsEnabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Functional Entity analyzing environmental data to adjust vehicle performance predictions and optimization recommendations."
    DTDFVocab:enables EnvironmentalImpactAnalysisService, PerformanceAdjustmentService
]

instance ManufacturingOptimizationEnabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Functional Entity implementing multi-objective optimization algorithms for manufacturing process improvements."
    DTDFVocab:enables ProcessOptimizationService, ResourceAllocationService
]

instance DigitalTwinEntity : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Central Digital Twin Entity managing data management, control logic, and model synchronization for the Observable Manufacturing Element."
    DTDFVocab:enables OperationalMonitoringService, PredictiveMaintenanceService, ClosedLoopControlService
]

instance UserInteractionEnabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Functional Entity providing human-interpretable interfaces and control panels for user interaction with the digital twin."
    DTDFVocab:enables VisualizationService, ControlInterfaceService, FeedbackIntegrationService
]

// C6: Services
instance ObjectTrackingService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Service tracking objects in real-time using processed LiDAR data for autonomous navigation and collision avoidance."
    DTDFVocab:provides ObjectTrackingInsight
]

instance PathProjectionService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Service projecting driving paths based on clustered object data for trajectory optimization."
    DTDFVocab:provides PathProjectionInsight
]

instance DataStandardizationService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Service standardizing heterogeneous data streams for consistent digital twin integration."
    DTDFVocab:provides DataStandardizationInsight
]

instance DataSerializationService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Service serializing standardized data for transmission to digital twin components."
    DTDFVocab:provides SerializationInsight
]

instance BatteryHealthMonitoringService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Service continuously monitoring battery health metrics and providing real-time status updates."
    DTDFVocab:provides BatteryHealthInsight
]

instance DegradationPredictionService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Service predicting battery degradation trends and remaining useful life based on state data."
    DTDFVocab:provides DegradationPredictionInsight
]

instance EnvironmentalImpactAnalysisService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Service analyzing how environmental conditions affect vehicle performance and suggesting adjustments."
    DTDFVocab:provides ImpactAnalysisInsight
]

instance PerformanceAdjustmentService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Service adjusting vehicle performance parameters based on environmental data analysis."
    DTDFVocab:provides PerformanceAdjustmentInsight
]

instance ProcessOptimizationService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Service optimizing manufacturing processes by adjusting parameters for improved yield and efficiency."
    DTDFVocab:provides OptimizationRecommendationInsight
]

instance ResourceAllocationService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Service allocating manufacturing resources based on optimization models and real-time constraints."
    DTDFVocab:provides AllocationRecommendationInsight
]

instance OperationalMonitoringService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Service providing real-time insights into equipment health, process efficiency, and operational anomalies."
    DTDFVocab:provides OperationalHealthInsight, EfficiencyInsight, AnomalyAlertInsight
]

instance PredictiveMaintenanceService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Service forecasting equipment failures and recommending maintenance schedules based on predictive analytics."
    DTDFVocab:provides FailureForecastInsight, MaintenanceScheduleInsight
]

instance ClosedLoopControlService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Service implementing closed-loop control by executing recommendations from the digital twin to physical systems."
    DTDFVocab:provides ControlCommandAction
]

instance VisualizationService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Service providing interactive 3D/4D visualizations of the digital twin and its operational data."
    DTDFVocab:provides VisualizationInsight
]

instance ControlInterfaceService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Service offering user interfaces for manual control and override of automated decisions."
    DTDFVocab:provides ControlInterfaceInsight
]

instance FeedbackIntegrationService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Service integrating user feedback into the digital twin for iterative improvement of insights."
    DTDFVocab:provides FeedbackIntegrationInsight
]

// C17: Insights/Actions
instance ObjectTrackingInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Real-time tracking of objects detected in the vehicle's environment for navigation and safety."
]

instance PathProjectionInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Projected driving paths based on object tracking data for trajectory optimization."
]

instance DataStandardizationInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Standardized data formats ensuring consistency across digital twin components."
]

instance SerializationInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Serialized data ready for transmission to digital twin processing components."
]

instance BatteryHealthInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Current state-of-charge and state-of-health metrics for battery systems."
]

instance DegradationPredictionInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Predicted degradation trends and remaining useful life for battery components."
]

instance ImpactAnalysisInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Analysis of how environmental conditions impact vehicle performance."
]

instance PerformanceAdjustmentInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Recommended adjustments to vehicle performance parameters based on environmental analysis."
]

instance OptimizationRecommendationInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Recommendations for adjusting manufacturing process parameters to improve efficiency."
]

instance AllocationRecommendationInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Suggested resource allocations for manufacturing operations based on optimization models."
]

instance OperationalHealthInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Real-time health status of manufacturing equipment and systems."
]

instance EfficiencyInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Current process efficiency metrics and performance benchmarks."
]

instance AnomalyAlertInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Alerts for detected operational anomalies requiring attention."
]

instance FailureForecastInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Predicted failure events and their projected timing."
]

instance MaintenanceScheduleInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Recommended maintenance schedules based on predictive analytics."
]

instance ControlCommandAction : DTDFVocab:Action [
    base:desc "Automated control commands executed by the digital twin to adjust physical systems in real-time."
    DTDFVocab:IsAutomatic true
]

instance VisualizationInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Interactive 3D/4D visual representations of the digital twin and its operational data."
]

instance ControlInterfaceInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "User interfaces for manual control and override of automated decisions."
]

instance FeedbackIntegrationInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Integrated user feedback mechanisms for iterative improvement of digital twin insights."
]

// C1: Entities
instance PhysicalTwinEntity : DTDFVocab:Entity [
    base:desc "Entity bridging physical resources (sensors/actuators) and digital services in the decentralized architecture."
    base:contains LidarSensor, MotorServoSensor, BatteryHealthSensor, EnvironmentalSensor
]

instance DigitalTwinEntity : DTDFVocab:Entity [
    base:desc "Central entity managing data management, control logic, and model synchronization for the Observable Manufacturing Element."
    base:contains PointCloudPreProcessingEnabler, DataTranslationEnabler, BatteryAnalyticsEnabler,
                EnvironmentalAnalyticsEnabler, ManufacturingOptimizationEnabler
]

instance UserEntity : DTDFVocab:Entity [
    base:desc "Entity providing visualization, control interfaces, and feedback integration for user interaction."
    base:contains UserInteractionEnabler
]

// C2: Acting Components
instance DataCollectionAndDeviceControlEntity : DTDFVocab:ActingComponent [
    base:desc "Component responsible for validating data integrity, accuracy, and consistency before ingestion by the Digital Twin Entity."
]

instance ControlPanel : DTDFVocab:ActingComponent [
    base:desc "User interface component allowing manual override and refinement of automated decisions."
]

// C5: Environments
instance AutonomousVehicleEnvironment : DTDFVocab:Environment [
    base:desc "Environment containing physical and digital components for autonomous vehicle systems including sensors, models, and control systems."
    base:contains LidarSensor, MotorServoSensor, BatteryHealthSensor, EnvironmentalSensor,
                PointCloudPreProcessingEnabler, VehicleStateModel, DigitalTwinEntity
]

instance ManufacturingEnvironment : DTDFVocab:Environment [
    base:desc "Environment containing physical manufacturing elements and their digital twins for process optimization and monitoring."
    base:contains BatteryHealthSensor, EnvironmentalSensor, ProcessOptimizationModel,
                ManufacturingOptimizationEnabler, OperationalMonitoringService
]

instance virtual_to_physical_interaction : DTDFVocab:VirtualToPhysical [
    base:desc "
The **Virtual-to-Physical Interaction** in this DTS is facilitated by the **Controlling FE (ACC)** and **Actuation FE**, enabling closed-loop control via command execution on physical actuators. **Command types** include **ACC (Adaptive Cruise Control) inputs**, which are serialized and translated through the **Data Translation FE** to align with physical actuator interfaces (e.g., motor, steering servo). **Control message formats** are standardized via the **Data Translation FE**, ensuring compatibility between digital twin logic and physical hardware. **Validation and authentication mechanisms** are not explicitly detailed, but the system implies reliance on **ROS2** for intra-component communication and **Wi-Fi/UDP** for inter-entity reliability, suggesting protocol-level integrity checks. **Feedback systems** are inferred through the **Data Collecting FE**, which captures post-actuation vehicle state data (e.g., motor/servo responses) for real-time adjustment in the digital twin. **Conflict resolution** is not explicitly described, but the **Controlling FE** likely prioritizes high-level ACC logic over lower-level actuation conflicts via centralized message routing. **Command execution confirmation** is implied through the **Actuation FE**, which processes translated commands and returns status updates to the digital twin for iterative control adjustments."
]

instance twinning_time_scale : DTDFVocab:TimeScale [
    base:desc "The Digital Twin system enforces a **100 ms clock synchronization accuracy** between physical and digital components to enable computation offloading and coordination, adhering to stricter vehicular application standards despite hardware limitations. Temporal granularity is dictated by the **ROS2-based execution framework**, where each Function Entity (FE) is implemented as an individual ROS2 package containing ROS2 nodes. Latency requirements for services are implicitly governed by the **ISO 23247 Reference Architecture (RA)**, which mandates real-time synchronization for safety-critical operations, though explicit service-level time scales (e.g., for data pre-processing, actuation, or Point Cloud Clustering & Tracking) are not detailed in the provided documents. The **Physical Twin Entity** handles time-sensitive operations like actuation and device communication, while the **Digital Twin Entity** manages control mechanisms with unspecified but likely lower-latency constraints compared to physical interactions."
]

instance dt_constellation : DTDFVocab:Constellation [
    base:desc "The Digital Twin system adopts a **ROS2-based microservices architecture**, where each Function Entity (FE) is implemented as an independent ROS2 package containing ROS2 nodes. This aligns with the **ISO 23247 RA’s entity-based structure**, but with deviations: the **Physical Twin Entity** extends beyond resource-specific operations to include general automotive functionalities (e.g., Point Cloud Clustering & Tracking), deviating from the OME’s scope. Orchestration is achieved through **ROS2’s publish-subscribe model**, enabling asynchronous communication between entities. Resource management is distributed across hardware components, with no centralized orchestrator explicitly mentioned. The system integrates **sensors/actuators** (Physical Twin Entity) with **data pre-processing** and **control mechanisms** (Digital Twin Entity), while the **User Entity** provides a singular **User Interface FE** split into 3D Visualization and Control Panel components. The architecture emphasizes **modularity and entity ownership**, impacting engineering processes due to functional deviations from ISO 23247."
]

instance life_cycle_stages : DTDFVocab:EvolutionStage [
    base:desc "
The lifecycle of the Digital Twin (DT) system, as defined in **ISO 23247 Part 2 (Reference Architecture)**, follows an **entity-based framework** structured around four primary roles: **Observable Manufacturing Element (OME)**, **Data Collection and Device Control Entity**, **Digital Twin Entity**, and **User Entity**. Each lifecycle phase is governed by **transition management** between these entities, ensuring seamless data flow and functional alignment.

1. **Development Phase**:
   - **OME Definition**: The lifecycle begins with identifying and defining **Observable Manufacturing Elements (OMEs)**, which include physical assets (equipment, facilities, products) and operational processes (personnel, workflows). These OMEs serve as the foundational observable units for the DT.
   - **Entity Decomposition**: The system decomposes OMEs into **Functional Entities (FEs)**, smaller modular components responsible for specific tasks (e.g., data actuation, human-interpretable transformation, or user interfacing). The standard does not prescribe implementation methods for FEs but suggests **OPC Unified Architecture (OPC UA)** as a potential protocol for interoperability.

2. **Representation Phases**:
   - **Physical-to-Digital Mapping**: The **Digital Twin Entity** creates a **digital representation** of the OME, maintaining a **bidirectional synchronization** with the physical counterpart. This representation evolves dynamically as the OME undergoes changes (e.g., upgrades, failures, or operational adjustments).
   - **Data Collection Phase**: The **Data Collection and Device Control Entity** continuously gathers real-time or historical data from the OME via sensors, IoT devices, or embedded systems. Data formats are standardized to ensure compatibility with the Digital Twin Entity’s processing requirements.

3. **Operation and Maintenance Phase**:
   - **Runtime Synchronization**: The DT operates in a **closed-loop system**, where the Digital Twin Entity updates its model based on data from the OME, while the Data Collection Entity adjusts control actions (e.g., actuating machinery) to maintain alignment with operational goals.
   - **Version Control**: Although not explicitly detailed in ISO 23247, the lifecycle implies **versioned representations** of the DT to track changes (e.g., software updates, hardware modifications) and ensure traceability. The standard emphasizes **functional consistency** across versions to avoid disruptions.

4. **Lifecycle Governance**:
   - **Entity Interoperability**: Governance is achieved through **modular entity interactions**, where each entity (OME, Data Collection, Digital Twin, User) adheres to defined interfaces. The standard does not mandate governance frameworks but highlights the need for **clear ownership** of each entity’s responsibilities.
   - **Transition Management**: Transitions between phases (e.g., from development to operation) are managed via **well-defined handoffs** between entities. For example, the Data Collection Entity must validate data integrity before passing it to the Digital Twin Entity for processing.

The lifecycle is **domain-specific to manufacturing**, with no explicit mention of broader industrial or non-manufacturing applications in the provided documents.
"
]

instance fidelity_and_validity_considerations : DTDFVocab:FidelityConsideration [
    base:desc "The **ISO 23247** standard implicitly outlines fidelity and validity considerations through its **entity-based RA**, where **Digital Twin Entities (DTEs)** must accurately reflect **Observable Manufacturing Elements (OMEs)**. While the document does not specify **validation/verification methods** (e.g., statistical testing, simulation benchmarking) or **uncertainty quantification techniques**, it implies that fidelity is ensured via **functional decomposition** into **Functional Entities (FEs)**—each responsible for localized tasks like data transformation or control. Error detection/correction mechanisms are not detailed, but the RA’s modular design suggests **entity-level error isolation** and **user-driven quality assurance** via the **User Entity** interface. No explicit mention of **model calibration, uncertainty propagation, or formal quality assurance processes** (e.g., ISO 9001 alignment) is provided."
]

instance dt_technical_connection : DTDFVocab:TechnicalConnection [
    base:desc "
The **Digital Twin Technical Connection** architecture leverages a **multi-layered communication framework** combining **ROS2** for intra-system SW component interactions and **Wi-Fi (UDP transport layer)** for inter-entity communication between Physical Twin, Digital Twin, and User Entities. **Network topology** follows a **distributed deployment**, where sensor data (LiDAR, motor/servo) propagates through **Data Pre-Processing FE** and **Actuation FE** before reaching the **Controlling FE** and **Visualization FE**. **Bandwidth and latency requirements** are critical for real-time operation, particularly for LiDAR point cloud processing and ACC control loops, though exact metrics (e.g., Mbps, ms latency) are not provided. **Reliability mechanisms** include **UDP-based redundancy** for inter-entity communication, ensuring continuous data flow despite transient network issues. **Security protocols** are not explicitly mentioned, but the use of **Wi-Fi/UDP** suggests reliance on standard IP-level security (e.g., encryption) rather than specialized DT-specific measures. The **Data Translation FE** acts as a critical bridge, serializing data streams and performing unit conversions to ensure cross-platform compatibility across ROS2 and Wi-Fi networks."
]

instance dt_hosting_deployment : DTDFVocab:Deployment [
    base:desc "
The **Digital Twin Hosting and Deployment** infrastructure is **edge-centric**, with components distributed across **Physical Twin (onboard vehicle)**, **Digital Twin (local/edge processing)**, and **User Entity (remote visualization/reporting)**. **Computational requirements** are met by the **Data Pre-Processing FE**, **Actuation FE**, and **Controlling FE**, which execute on-board or edge devices capable of handling real-time LiDAR clustering, object tracking, and ACC logic. **Storage requirements** are implied for **Data Storage FE**, which archives processed data (e.g., reports, vehicle state logs) and raw point clouds for post-processing. **Scalability mechanisms** are not explicitly detailed, but the modular **ROS2-based SW architecture** allows for horizontal scaling of components (e.g., additional Data Collecting/Reporting FEs) as needed. **Deployment models supported** include **on-premise (vehicle-mounted)** and **edge (local processing hubs)** but do not extend to cloud-based deployment. **Availability/reliability assurance** relies on **Wi-Fi/UDP redundancy** and **ROS2 fault tolerance**, ensuring continuous operation during network fluctuations or component failures. The system does not mention cloud integration or hybrid deployment strategies."
]

instance standardization : DTDFVocab:Standardization [
    base:desc "
The Digital Twin (DT) system described in **ISO 23247** adheres to a **comprehensive standardization framework**, primarily structured around **industry-specific standards, interoperability protocols, and modular entity-based architectures**. The standard defines compliance requirements, interoperability guidelines, and certification implications for manufacturing-focused DTs.

1. **Relevant Industry Standards**:
   - **ISO 23247**: The foundational standard for DTs in manufacturing, divided into four parts:
     - **Part 1**: Overview and general principles (not detailed in the provided context).
     - **Part 2**: **Reference Architecture (RA)**, which defines the **entity-based framework** (OME, Data Collection, Digital Twin, User Entities) and their interactions. This is the most relevant part for implementation.
     - **Part 3**: Digital representation of physical manufacturing elements (not detailed here).
     - **Part 4**: Information exchange standards (not detailed here).
   - **OPC Unified Architecture (OPC UA)**: While not explicitly mandated by ISO 23247, the standard **suggests OPC UA** as a protocol for **interoperability between Functional Entities (FEs)** and other system components. OPC UA ensures **secure, platform-independent data exchange** and is widely adopted in industrial automation.

2. **Specifications Followed**:
   - **Entity-Based Modularity**: The DT system follows a **modular specification**, where each entity (OME, Data Collection, Digital Twin, User) is designed to **implement specific functionalities** without tight coupling. This aligns with **service-oriented architecture (SOA)** principles.
   - **Data Collection Standards**: The **Data Collection and Device Control Entity** must adhere to **industrial data standards** (e.g., OPC UA, MQTT) for **real-time data acquisition** and **device control**. The standard does not specify exact protocols but emphasizes **standardized interfaces**.
   - **Digital Representation Standards**: The **Digital Twin Entity** must maintain a **consistent digital representation** of the OME, adhering to **semantic and syntactic standards** for interoperability. This includes **data modeling** (e.g., using **ISO 15926** for product lifecycle data, though not explicitly mentioned).

3. **Certification Requirements**:
   - **ISO 23247 Compliance**: While not explicitly stated, adherence to **ISO 23247** implies **certification pathways** for DT systems in manufacturing. Compliance ensures **interoperability, reliability, and traceability** of the DT’s lifecycle.
   - **Functional Validation**: The standard implies **certification of functional entities** (FEs) to verify their performance, interoperability, and alignment with the DT’s overall architecture. No specific certification bodies or processes are mentioned.
   - **Not in Document**: No details are provided on **third-party certification** (e.g., ISO 9001, IEC 62443) or **regulatory compliance** (e.g., GDPR for data handling).

4. **Compliance Frameworks**:
   - **Entity Governance**: Compliance is managed through **clear governance of each entity’s responsibilities**, ensuring that:
     - The **Data Collection Entity** adheres to **data integrity and security standards**.
     - The **Digital Twin Entity** maintains **model accuracy and consistency**.
     - The **User Entity** provides **secure and intuitive interfaces**.
   - **Interoperability Frameworks**: The standard emphasizes **interoperability between entities** via **standardized interfaces** (e.g., OPC UA). This ensures seamless data flow and **cross-system compatibility**.

5. **Interoperability Standards Adopted**:
   - **OPC UA**: The primary interoperability standard suggested for **data exchange and device control** between FEs and other entities. OPC UA supports **real-time synchronization**, **security**, and **platform independence**.
   - **Modular Interfaces**: The DT’s architecture relies on **well-defined interfaces** for each entity, enabling **plug-and-play compatibility** with other systems. This aligns with **industrial IoT (IIoT) interoperability standards**.
   - **Not in Document**: No mention of **cloud interoperability standards** (e.g., OASIS, DDS), **edge computing standards**, or **semantic interoperability frameworks** (e.g., ontologies like **OWL, RDF**).
}"
]

instance security_and_safety_considerations : DTDFVocab:SecuritySafety [
    base:desc "
The Digital Twin system described in the provided documents incorporates **ISO 26262-1:2018** functional safety standards for **road vehicles**, mandating rigorous cybersecurity and safety protocols. Key measures include:
- **Cybersecurity Protocols**: Adherence to **ISO 26262-8** (automotive-specific cybersecurity requirements) for secure communication and data integrity, ensuring protection against unauthorized access and tampering in **V2X (Vehicle-to-Everything) services** (as referenced in **3GPP TS 22.185 v15.0.0**).
- **Access Control Mechanisms**: Role-based access control (RBAC) and **authentication systems** (e.g., **EAP-AKA’** for V2X services) to enforce granular permissions for system components, as outlined in **3GPP TS 22.185**.
- **Threat Mitigation Strategies**: Implementation of **fail-safe mechanisms** (e.g., redundant system checks, graceful degradation) to comply with **ISO 26262-4** for safety-critical systems, ensuring continuous operation under fault conditions.
- **Risk Assessment Approaches**: Systematic **risk analysis** (e.g., **FMEA - Failure Mode and Effects Analysis**) per **ISO 26262-5** to identify vulnerabilities in the Digital Twin’s **interactive cockpit models** (as discussed in **Dalibor et al., 2020**), prioritizing mitigation for high-severity threats.
- **Safety-Critical System Considerations**: Integration of **safety mechanisms** (e.g., watchdog timers, hardware redundancy) to prevent system failures from propagating, aligned with **ISO 26262-6** for automotive-grade reliability. The **model-driven architecture** (MDA) proposed in **Dalibor et al., 2020** emphasizes **safety-by-design** principles for Digital Twin cockpits, ensuring compliance with **ISO 26262-8** for cyber-physical system security.
"
]
